page: Microsoft Azure D1_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv2 server family. It is equipped with a single dedicated x86_64 vCPU from an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory and 51 GB of local storage. The CPU features a 50 MB L3 cache, 32 KB L1d cache, and 0.25 MB L2 cache, with support for AVX, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instruction sets.

Benchmark evaluations place the D1_v2 in the lower performance tier, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU metrics scoring at 741.48. Memory bandwidth tests show 60.99 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 6.55 GB/sec for uncached large block reads and 4.81 GB/sec for writes. Geekbench 5 scores reflect this entry-level profile, yielding a single-core score of 774.0 and a multi-core score of 796.0. Database operations via Redis achieve 0.92 million SET operations per second, while static web serving throughput is measured at 0.89 GB/sec.

Given its single-core architecture and entry-level benchmark results, this instance represents a low-resource entry point within the Azure ecosystem. It provides a dedicated CPU core and a modest memory footprint without the overhead of high-tier compute or GPU accelerators. The D1_v2 is suited for lightweight, non-intensive workloads such as basic web serving, small-scale development or testing environments, simple scripting tasks, and low-traffic background services that do not require high parallel processing capabilities.
description: The Microsoft Azure D1_v2 is an entry-level, general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv2 family. Powered by a single dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz, it features 4.0 GB of RAM and 51 GB of local storage. Its x86_64 architecture supports AVX, AES, and FMA instructions. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tier, with a Geekbench single-core score of 774.0 and multi-core score of 796.0, alongside a static web serving throughput of 0.89 GB/sec. Lacking GPU acceleration and high-speed multi-core capabilities, the D1_v2 is designed for low-demand workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, lightweight development environments, simple scripting, and small-scale background tasks.

tagline: An entry-level single-core virtual machine with dedicated compute resources and local storage for lightweight development and basic web serving.
